SqlAuthenticationProviderManager.summary: Manages authentication provider registration for SQL Server connections. Provides methods to get and set instances for specific values.

remarks.para: The and methods are AOT-safe because they do not use reflection — providers are registered via direct static method calls.

By default, the manager's static constructor performs reflection-based discovery of the Azure authentication extension assembly. This is safe for JIT applications but incompatible with NativeAOT trimming. To disable this discovery and make the manager fully AOT-compatible, set the Microsoft.Data.SqlClient.EnableReflectionBasedAuthenticationProviderDiscovery runtime configuration option to at publish time, and register providers explicitly using .

ApplicationClientId.summary: Gets the application client ID configured via the SqlClientAuthenticationProviders or SqlAuthenticationProviders configuration section in app.config.
ApplicationClientId.value: The configured application client ID, or if no client ID was configured.
ApplicationClientId.remarks: This value can be passed to authentication provider constructors (such as ActiveDirectoryAuthenticationProvider) when registering providers explicitly in AOT applications.

GetProvider.summary: Gets the authentication provider registered for the specified authentication method.
GetProvider.param: The authentication method to look up.
GetProvider.returns: The registered for the specified method, or if no provider is registered.

SetProvider.summary: Registers an authentication provider for the specified authentication method.
SetProvider.param: The authentication method to register a provider for.
SetProvider.exception: Thrown if the provider does not support the specified authentication method.

remarks.para: Providers registered via application configuration (app.config) cannot be replaced by calling this method. In that case, this method returns .

If a provider was previously registered for the same method (not via app.config), it will be replaced and its method will be called before the new provider's
method is invoked.
